items.title: 'That is our challenge: to make politics work': Andy Burnham becomes UK PM
items.standfirst: Andy Burnham has become Britain's new prime minister. The nomination process unfolded on July 20th in London, with one outgoing Labour leader passing the baton to another. The hope is that Burnham will ease the leadership turnover, stabilising the country.
items.content: Andy Burnham has become Britain's new prime minister. The nomination process unfolded on July 20th in London, with one outgoing Labour leader passing the baton to another. The hope is that Burnham will ease the leadership turnover, stabilising the country.

items.title: Andy Burnham: Who is the new UK prime minister?
items.standfirst: 🇬🇧 Andy Burnham succeeds Keir #Starmer as UK Prime Minister. The former #Manchester mayor has pledged to tackle the cost of living, expand devolution and reform the social care system.
items.content: 🇬🇧 Andy Burnham succeeds Keir #Starmer as UK Prime Minister. The former #Manchester mayor has pledged to tackle the cost of living, expand devolution and reform the social care system.

items.title: Andy Burnham touts plans after becoming 7th prime minister in a decade
items.standfirst: Andy Burnham addressed crowds outside 10 Downing Street on Monday, where he touted his plans for the U.K. after becoming Britain's seventh prime minister since 2016. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab has more.
items.content: Andy Burnham addressed crowds outside 10 Downing Street on Monday, where he touted his plans for the U.K. after becoming Britain's seventh prime minister since 2016. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab has more.

items.title: Andy Burnham: Who is the new PM?
items.standfirst: From Manchester mayor to prime minister; who is he and what does stand for?
items.content: From Manchester mayor to prime minister; who is he and what does stand for?

items.title: After getting a new PM, the UK waits for his government and first policies
items.standfirst: Former Manchester mayor Andy Burnham has become the UK's new prime minister, the seventh in a decade, following Keir Starmer's resignation on July 20. In his first speech, he has vowed to start a ten-year plan for Britain. His government and his first policies will quickly be announced. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more.
items.content: Former Manchester mayor Andy Burnham has become the UK's new prime minister, the seventh in a decade, following Keir Starmer's resignation on July 20. In his first speech, he has vowed to start a ten-year plan for Britain. His government and his first policies will quickly be announced. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more.

items.title: What to know about Andy Burnham's first speech as prime minister
items.standfirst: The UK has a new prime minister. Former Manchester mayor Andy Burnham is starting his premiership following Keir Starmer's resignation on July 20. In his first speech since officially becoming prime minister, he has promised a 10-year plan for the country. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot takes a look.
items.content: The UK has a new prime minister. Former Manchester mayor Andy Burnham is starting his premiership following Keir Starmer's resignation on July 20. In his first speech since officially becoming prime minister, he has promised a 10-year plan for the country. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot takes a look.

items.title: What is outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer's legacy?
items.standfirst: Keir Starmer leaves Downing Street Monday, July 20th, passing the keys to former Mayor of Greater Manchester, Andy Burnham. He came to victory with a huge majority in 2024, but just two years later his popularity ratings had sunk to record lows.
items.content: Keir Starmer leaves Downing Street Monday, July 20th, passing the keys to former Mayor of Greater Manchester, Andy Burnham. He came to victory with a huge majority in 2024, but just two years later his popularity ratings had sunk to record lows.

items.title: Watch: Starmer resigns, Burnham becomes PM... the day in 101 seconds
items.standfirst: Andy Burnham has become the UK's prime minister after being appointed by the King at Buckingham Palace.
items.content: Andy Burnham has become the UK's prime minister after being appointed by the King at Buckingham Palace.

items.title: 🔴Watch live: UK’s new Prime Minister Andy Burnham speaks outside 10 Downing Street
Switch in UK prime minister comes amid growing voter scepticism
items.standfirst: UK soon-to-be Prime Minister Andy Burnham has vowed to 'bring back hope' to the country as he succeeds to Keir Starmer. He is becoming the UK's seventh prime minister in a decade, and the fourth one since 2022. His biggest challenge will be to convince voters to trust Labour can bring stability to the country.
items.content: UK soon-to-be Prime Minister Andy Burnham has vowed to 'bring back hope' to the country as he succeeds to Keir Starmer. He is becoming the UK's seventh prime minister in a decade, and the fourth one since 2022. His biggest challenge will be to convince voters to trust Labour can bring stability to the country.

items.title: Andy Burnham wants to lead the UK 'differently': how different can he be?
items.standfirst: Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester and new leader of the Labour Party, is taking the final steps to become prime minister and succeed to Keir Starmer. While he has vowed to do things differently to his predecessor, he is facing the same challenges. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot looks at what he will be facing.
items.content: Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester and new leader of the Labour Party, is taking the final steps to become prime minister and succeed to Keir Starmer. While he has vowed to do things differently to his predecessor, he is facing the same challenges. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot looks at what he will be facing.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes seventh British prime minister in a decade
items.standfirst: The new Labour Party leader Andy Burnham is set to replace Keir Starmer as British prime minister, inheriting a country beset by low wages and internal strife.
items.content: The new Labour Party leader Andy Burnham is set to replace Keir Starmer as British prime minister, inheriting a country beset by low wages and internal strife.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. prime minister
items.standfirst: In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham has replaced Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party.

items.title: Mexican drug lord ‘El Mayo’ sentenced to life in prison in US federal court
items.standfirst: Case against Ismael Zambada García, who helped co-found and lead the violent Sinaloa cartel, unfolded in New YorkThe Mexican drug kingpin, Ismael Zambada García, known as “El Mayo”, was sentenced in a US federal court on Monday to life in prison, after a plea deal last year in which he admitted ⁠to shipping vast amounts of cocaine and other substances as leader of the violent Sinaloa cartel.Zambada is 76 and one of the most high-profile Mexican ⁠drug traffickers to be sentenced in a US court since his fellow Sinaloa leader Joaquín “El Chapo” Guzmán ​was sentenced to life in prison in ‌2019. The case unfolded in federal court in Brooklyn, New York. Continue reading...
items.content: Case against Ismael Zambada García, who helped co-found and lead the violent Sinaloa cartel, unfolded in New YorkThe Mexican drug kingpin, Ismael Zambada García, known as “El Mayo”, was sentenced in a US federal court on Monday to life in prison, after a plea deal last year in which he admitted ⁠to shipping vast amounts of cocaine and other substances as leader of the violent Sinaloa cartel.Zambada is 76 and one of the most high-profile Mexican ⁠drug traffickers to be sentenced in a US court since his fellow Sinaloa leader Joaquín “El Chapo” Guzmán ​was sentenced to life in prison in ‌2019. The case unfolded in federal court in Brooklyn, New York. Continue reading...

items.content: They were arrested on Saturday after the UK announced dozens of new charges, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny.

items.title: Tate brothers fight extradition to UK over rape and sex-trafficking charges
items.standfirst: Polarizing social media influencers are being held in federal detention in Miami as district judge considers extraditionSocial media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the United Kingdom, their lawyer told a US magistrate in Miami on Monday after the brothers were arrested to face rape and sex-trafficking charges.The Tates appeared in federal court for the first time since their arrest on Saturday and are being held in federal detention. In the coming weeks, a US district judge will consider whether they meet the conditions for extradition. Continue reading...
items.content: Polarizing social media influencers are being held in federal detention in Miami as district judge considers extraditionSocial media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the United Kingdom, their lawyer told a US magistrate in Miami on Monday after the brothers were arrested to face rape and sex-trafficking charges.The Tates appeared in federal court for the first time since their arrest on Saturday and are being held in federal detention. In the coming weeks, a US district judge will consider whether they meet the conditions for extradition. Continue reading...

items.content: Andrew and Tristan Tate appeared in a Miami courtroom after British prosecutors unveiled a wave of new charges, including rape. Authorities from the U.K. are seeking to extradite the Tate brothers. NBC News' Danny Cevallos reports on the 38 charges they face.

Reported by Al Jazeera, BBC World, Guardian World and others

items.title: ‘Now or never’: Students defy Delhi lockdown to march on India’s parliament
items.standfirst: Youth-led protesters answer call of Cockroach Janta Party to demand accountability, sweeping reforms after exam scandal.
items.content: Youth-led protesters answer call of Cockroach Janta Party to demand accountability, sweeping reforms after exam scandal.

items.title: Police block thousands of 'cockroach' protesters marching on India's parliament
items.standfirst: Some protesters demanding education reforms said they were injured in clashes with police who banned the march.
items.content: Some protesters demanding education reforms said they were injured in clashes with police who banned the march.

items.title: Tens of thousands of Cockroach movement protesters defy police with Delhi march
items.standfirst: Youth-led group calls on education minister to quit in one of India’s largest anti-government rallies in yearsTens of thousands of protesters have defied police orders and descended on Delhi to demand the resignation of India’s education minister in one of the largest anti-government demonstrations in years.A huge crowd, largely made up of students and gen Z-ers, filled the streets in the heart of India’s capital from early Monday morning shouting anti-government slogans. Hundreds had already camped out overnight. Continue reading...
items.content: Youth-led group calls on education minister to quit in one of India’s largest anti-government rallies in yearsTens of thousands of protesters have defied police orders and descended on Delhi to demand the resignation of India’s education minister in one of the largest anti-government demonstrations in years.A huge crowd, largely made up of students and gen Z-ers, filled the streets in the heart of India’s capital from early Monday morning shouting anti-government slogans. Hundreds had already camped out overnight. Continue reading...

items.title: Why are thousands of people protesting in Delhi?
items.standfirst: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.
items.content: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.

items.title: India’s Cockroach Janta Party movement grows as thousands of protesters gather
items.standfirst: India’s Cockroach Janta Party movement grows as thousands of protesters gather for a march demanding education reforms. The cockroach movement began after national medical school entrance exams were leaked.
items.content: India’s Cockroach Janta Party movement grows as thousands of protesters gather for a march demanding education reforms. The cockroach movement began after national medical school entrance exams were leaked.

items.title: India's Cockroach party leads thousands of youth in protest to parliament
items.standfirst: Demonstrators in the Indian capital have marched to parliament as they demand the sacking of the country's education minister. The youth-led Cockroach movement, started as a satirical youth platform, but has morphed into something larger. Protesters are more galvanised after a hunger-striking social activist was forcibly moved to hospital, against his will.
items.content: Demonstrators in the Indian capital have marched to parliament as they demand the sacking of the country's education minister. The youth-led Cockroach movement, started as a satirical youth platform, but has morphed into something larger. Protesters are more galvanised after a hunger-striking social activist was forcibly moved to hospital, against his will.

items.title: From hundreds to thousands: How India's 'cockroach' protest movement has grown
items.standfirst: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.
items.content: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.

items.title: US military identifies two soldiers killed in Iranian attack on Jordan
items.standfirst: Defense department says 1Lt Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and Pvt Isabella Gonzales of Texas were killedThe US military on Monday identified two soldiers killed in action recently as part of the war with Iran.The defense department said in a statement that 1Lt Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and Pvt Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas, were killed in Jordan, where they were supporting a US mission against the Islamic State group. It is unclear how they were killed. Continue reading...
items.content: Defense department says 1Lt Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and Pvt Isabella Gonzales of Texas were killedThe US military on Monday identified two soldiers killed in action recently as part of the war with Iran.The defense department said in a statement that 1Lt Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and Pvt Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas, were killed in Jordan, where they were supporting a US mission against the Islamic State group. It is unclear how they were killed. Continue reading...

items.content: The Pentagon on Monday released the names of two soldiers killed in Iranian strikes in Jordan, identifying them as 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales of Texas.

items.standfirst: Three people reported injured after incendiary device allegedly thrown at building also housing ICE field officeAuthorities in New York City are investigating an explosion on Monday morning outside an FBI building in lower Manhattan that reportedly injured three people.Investigators said a person who allegedly threw an incendiary device at the scene was taken into custody. The FBI later released the name in a press conference, identifying the suspect as Andrew Arrabaca. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.

clusters.title: At least six dead after earthquake hits Peru's Andes

bullet_summary: 5-magnitude earthquake shook the Andes region of Peru, killing at least six people, authorities said Sunday

items.title: At least six dead after earthquake hits Peru's Andes
items.standfirst: In the Andes, a 5.5 magnitude earthquake has hit Peru. At least six people died in the quake and more than 30 have been injured, according to local authorities. Damage was compounded by the area's rustic constructions - adding to that that the earthquake's epicenter was close to the surface.
items.content: In the Andes, a 5.5 magnitude earthquake has hit Peru. At least six people died in the quake and more than 30 have been injured, according to local authorities. Damage was compounded by the area's rustic constructions - adding to that that the earthquake's epicenter was close to the surface.

items.title: Earthquake in Peru leaves at least six dead
items.standfirst: More than 30 injured and 300 displaced after buildings collapse near SicayaA 5.5-magnitude earthquake shook the Andes region of Peru, killing at least six people, authorities said Sunday. More than 30 people were injured and 300 have been displaced.The US Geological Survey reported the quake struck on Saturday at 9.24pm, with its epicentre located 2km (1.2 miles) west-south-west of the city of Sicaya in Huancayo province. It was 10km (6.2 miles) deep. Continue reading...

items.title: Middle East crisis: US military says service member killed during ‘controlled detonation’ of downed Iranian drone – as it happened
items.standfirst: This live blog is now closed.US says another troop killed as Israel warns Tehran’s attacks coming closeTwo US troops killed and one missing in Jordan after Iranian attacksJordanian authorities ⁠have not issued any decision ⁠to ​evacuate the airport or ⁠seaport in the city of Aqaba, and have not detected ⁠any potential threats ​in ‌the past ‌hours, the state ‌news agency cited the government spokesperson as saying.This denial comes in response to the US embassy in Jordan earlier saying the airport and seaport in Aqaba had been evacuated by Jordanian authorities because of a “specific and credible threat” (see this post for more details). Continue reading...

items.title: New US military death as Israel warns Tehran’s attacks coming close
items.standfirst: US military says service member was killed in Iraq during ‘controlled detonation’ as it launches more airstrikes at IranMiddle East crisis live: follow latest updatesThe US military announced another death of a service member as it launched more airstrikes at Iran on Sunday in response to the killing of American troops, and Iran fired missiles toward Jordan that risked widening the conflict into neighboring Israel.The US military said the service member was killed in Iraq on Saturday during the “controlled detonation” of a downed Iranian drone. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: U.S. service member killed in Northern Iraq, says U.S. Central Command
items.standfirst: The U.S. military announced it recovered unidentified human remains while searching for an American service member reported missing after an Iranian missile and drone attack in Jordan, as another U.S. service member was killed in a separate incident in Iraq. NBC News' Julie Tsirkin and Keir Simmons report.
items.content: The U.S. military announced it recovered unidentified human remains while searching for an American service member reported missing after an Iranian missile and drone attack in Jordan, as another U.S. service member was killed in a separate incident in Iraq. NBC News' Julie Tsirkin and Keir Simmons report.
